# Executor Workflow

**For**: Cline execution agent  
**Triggered by**: Any task generated by the Claude Code planning system  
**Role boundary**: Cline implements. Claude Code plans. Never cross.

---

## Quick Reference

```
Phase 1 → Load context (story + task only)
Phase 2 → Validate scope (check allowed files + deps)
Phase 3 → Implement (strict scope, minimal edits)
Phase 4 → Validate (lint/test affected files only) [DISABLED by default — skip unless user says "validate"]
Phase 5 → Update context (append-only writes)
Phase 6 → Report completion (one structured block)
```

Stop after Phase 6. Do not continue to the next task.

---

## Phase 1 — Context Loading

**Objective**: Load exactly what the task requires. Nothing more.

**Load these files (in order):**

1. `.ai/stories/STORY-NNN-<slug>/story.md` — read the goal and constraints
2. `.ai/stories/STORY-NNN-<slug>/context.md` — read shared artifacts and prior state
3. `.ai/stories/STORY-NNN-<slug>/tasks/TASK-NNN-<slug>.md` — read the current task

**Only load additional files if the task explicitly names them** in its "Allowed Files" section.

**FORBIDDEN in this phase:**

- Do not scan the project tree
- Do not read files outside the allowed scope
- Do not load sibling tasks or adjacent stories
- Do not read config files unless listed in the task

> If a required file is missing → go to Failure Handling. Do not guess.

---

## Phase 2 — Scope Validation

**Objective**: Confirm the task is executable before writing a single line.

**Check all of the following:**

| Check | Pass condition |
|---|---|
| Allowed files listed | Task has an explicit "Allowed Files" section |
| `[modify]` files exist | Every `[modify]` entry exists on disk — if missing, STOP and report |
| `[create]` files absent | Every `[create]` entry does NOT yet exist — if it exists, treat as `[modify]`, note in log |
| Parent dirs reachable | For each `[create]` file, its parent directory exists OR the Objective says to create it |
| `[delete]` confirmed | Every `[delete]` entry triggers a user confirmation prompt — do NOT proceed until user says yes |
| Dependencies available | All files/modules listed in "Dependencies" exist on disk |
| No architecture conflict | Task does not require redesigning existing systems |
| Scope is bounded | Task touches ≤ 5 files total (warn if more) |
| No forbidden areas touched | Task does not touch files outside its Allowed Files list |

**If any check fails** → go to Failure Handling. Do not proceed.

**FORBIDDEN in this phase:**

- Do not expand scope to fix a related issue you notice
- Do not add files to the allowed list on your own

---

## Phase 3 — Implementation

**Objective**: Execute the task exactly as specified.

**Rules:**

- Only write to files in the task's "Allowed Files" list. The marker controls how:
  - `[modify]` — read the existing file first, then edit only the relevant section. Preserve all unrelated exports.
  - `[create]` — create the file from scratch using the content prescribed in Requirements. If the file already exists at run time, treat it as `[modify]` and preserve existing exports; record the deviation in the Context Update.
  - `[delete]` — **STOP before deleting.** Ask the user:
    ```
    This task requires deleting `<path>`. Confirm deletion? (yes / no)
    ```
    Proceed only on explicit "yes". If the user says no, record the skip in
    the Context Update and continue with the remaining Allowed Files.
- **Directory scaffolding**: if the Objective says "create parent directory `<dir>/`", create it before writing the file. Do not create directories that are not mentioned.
- Follow the task's acceptance criteria as the definition of done
- Make the minimal edit required — no cleanup, no polish, no extras
- If the task specifies exact types/signatures/names, use them verbatim
- If the task omits a detail, use the simplest valid implementation

**FORBIDDEN:**

- Refactoring code outside the task's scope
- Renaming files, functions, or variables not listed in the task
- Adding dependencies not listed in the task
- Introducing new abstractions not required by the task
- Improving unrelated code you happen to read

**If blocked mid-implementation** (missing type, conflicting interface, etc.):
→ Stop. Record the blocker. Go to Failure Handling.  
→ Do not improvise an architecture change to unblock yourself.

---

## Phase 4 — Validation

> **DISABLED by default.** Skip this phase entirely unless the user explicitly requests validation (e.g. "validate", "run lint", "check types"). When skipped, record `Validation: SKIPPED` in the Phase 6 report and execution log.

**Objective**: Confirm the implementation is correct for the affected scope.

**Run only what is relevant:**

```bash
# Lint changed files only (if applicable)
<lint-command> <changed-files>

# Type-check (project-wide is acceptable)
<typecheck-command>

# Run tests scoped to the task domain (if tests exist)
<test-command> --testPathPattern=<relevant-path>

# Build smoke check (only if the task touches routing or config)
<build-command>
```

See `.ai/architecture.md § Commands` for the exact commands for this project.

**FORBIDDEN:**

- Do not run full test suites unless the task requires it
- Do not run migrations or seed scripts unless explicitly listed
- Do not start the dev server to manually verify unless instructed

**If validation fails:**
→ Fix only errors caused by the current task's changes.  
→ Do not fix pre-existing lint warnings or unrelated failures.  
→ If a failure is outside task scope → record it and continue to Phase 5.

---

## Phase 5 — Context Update

**Objective**: Record what happened so the next session can resume accurately.

**Append to `.ai/stories/STORY-NNN/context.md`:**

```markdown
## TASK-NNN — <Task Title> [COMPLETED: YYYY-MM-DD]

**Files created:**

- `path/to/new-file.ts` — <one-line description of what was created>

**Files modified:**

- `path/to/file.ts` — <one-line description of change>

**Decisions made:**

- <any non-obvious choice, e.g. "used X instead of Y because Z">

**Exports added / changed:**

- `ExportName` in `path/to/file.ts` — <type signature>

**Known limitations:**

- <anything incomplete or deferred>

**Pending follow-ups:**

- <anything the next task must know>
```

**Append to `.ai/stories/STORY-NNN/logs/execution-log.md`:**

```markdown
### TASK-NNN [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM]

- Status: COMPLETED | FAILED | BLOCKED
- Files touched: <list>
- Validation: PASSED | FAILED (reason)
- Blocker (if any): <description>
```

**Rules:**

- These files are append-only. Never overwrite prior entries.
- Create `logs/` directory if it does not exist.
- Keep entries brief. One line per file. One line per decision.

---

## Phase 6 — Completion Report

**Objective**: Emit a single structured summary so the human can confirm and move on.

```
TASK COMPLETE: TASK-NNN — <Task Title>

Files created:
  - <path> (new file)

Files modified:
  - <path> (<what changed>)

Validation:
  - build: PASSED / SKIPPED
  - lint: PASSED / N/A / SKIPPED
  - tests: PASSED / N/A / SKIPPED

Context updated:
  - context.md: appended
  - execution-log.md: appended

Next task: TASK-NNN+1 — <Title> (waiting for human trigger)
```

**Stop here.** Do not load the next task. Do not continue autonomously.

---

## Failure Handling

When execution cannot proceed safely, stop and report. Do not improvise.

### Failure types and responses

| Failure | Response |
|---|---|
| Missing dependency file | Report exact missing path. Stop. |
| Unclear requirement | Quote the ambiguous line. Ask one specific question. Stop. |
| Architecture conflict | Describe the conflict. Do not resolve it. Escalate to Claude Code. |
| Validation failure (in-scope) | Fix. Re-run. Report. |
| Validation failure (out-of-scope) | Record in log. Continue to Phase 5. Do not fix. |
| Forbidden file touched accidentally | Revert the change. Report. Stop. |
| `[delete]` — user says no | Skip the deletion. Record in Context Update under "Decisions made". Continue remaining steps. |

### Failure report format

```
TASK BLOCKED: TASK-NNN — <Task Title>

Phase: <which phase failed>
Reason: <one clear sentence>
Affected file (if any): <path>
Recommended action: <one of: fix task definition / add missing dep / escalate to Claude Code>

Context updated: execution-log.md appended with BLOCKED status
```

---

## Safety Constraints

These constraints apply at all times and override task instructions.

1. **Never write to files outside the task's Allowed Files list.** `[create]` entries authorize creating new files; `[modify]` entries authorize editing existing files. Both are hard write boundaries.
2. **Never make architecture decisions.** If a structural choice is needed, stop and report.
3. **Never add dependencies without explicit task approval.**
4. **Never delete files** without asking the user first, even when the task carries a `[delete]` marker. Deletion requires confirmation at both planning time (task-creator asked) and execution time (executor asks again).
5. **Never rename files** unless the task explicitly says "rename `<old>` to `<new>`".
6. **Never run destructive commands** (drop tables, reset data, force-push) without explicit instruction.
7. **Context files are append-only.** Do not overwrite `context.md` or `execution-log.md`.
8. **Stop after one task.** Do not chain tasks autonomously.

---

## Operational Checklist

Run this mentally before starting Phase 3:

```
[ ] story.md loaded — I understand the story goal
[ ] context.md loaded — I know what prior tasks produced
[ ] task file loaded — I have the acceptance criteria
[ ] Allowed Files list is present in the task
[ ] All [modify] files exist on disk
[ ] All [create] files do NOT yet exist on disk (if they do, I will treat as [modify] and log the deviation)
[ ] For each [create] file: parent directory exists, OR Objective instructs me to create it
[ ] If any [delete] files are listed: I have NOT deleted anything yet — I will ask the user before each one
[ ] All dependencies listed in the task exist on disk
[ ] I have not loaded any files outside the allowed set
[ ] No architecture decisions are required to complete this task
[ ] I know exactly when I am done (acceptance criteria are clear)
```

If any box is unchecked → resolve it before implementing.

---

## Examples

### Minimal task flow (happy path)

```
Task: TASK-002 — Create <Entity>Repository class
Allowed: src/lib/<entity>-repository.ts (create)
Depends on: src/lib/db-client.ts (exists ✓), <db-schema-file> (exists ✓)

Phase 1: Load story.md + context.md + TASK-002.md
Phase 2: Validate — all deps exist, 1 file allowed ✓
Phase 3: Create src/lib/<entity>-repository.ts with specified methods
Phase 4: <typecheck-command> → PASSED
Phase 5: Append to context.md and execution-log.md
Phase 6: Report TASK COMPLETE
→ STOP
```

### Blocked task flow

```
Task: TASK-003 — Create GET /api/<entities> route
Allowed: src/app/api/<entity>/route.ts (create)
Depends on: src/lib/<entity>-repository.ts ← FILE DOES NOT EXIST

Phase 1: Load files
Phase 2: Validate — dependency missing ✗

→ STOP
Report: "TASK BLOCKED: TASK-003. Missing dependency: src/lib/<entity>-repository.ts.
         TASK-002 must be completed first."
Append BLOCKED status to execution-log.md
```

---

## Scaling Notes

This workflow is intentionally minimal. As the project grows:

- **More stories**: The workflow scales unchanged — each task is isolated.
- **Shared utilities**: Always declare them in `context.md` Shared Artifacts. Never assume.
- **Larger teams**: The append-only log in `execution-log.md` provides a full audit trail.
- **Parallel tracks**: Each story has its own `context.md` — parallel stories do not share state.
- **Long stories (10+ tasks)**: Split the story via Claude Code before executing. Do not split during execution.

The workflow does not need to change as the codebase scales. Only the number of story directories grows.
